name: Paraguayan Corn Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease a 13x9x2-inch baking pan and sprinkle with 1 tablespoon flour.
2. Shake off excess flour.
3. In a skillet, sauté onions in 4 tablespoons margarine until softened.
4. Add farmer cheese; blend thoroughly.
5. Stir in Muenster cheese, corn, salt, milk, and cornmeal until well combined.
6. Beat egg whites until soft peaks form, then beat yolks separately.
7. Combine the beaten egg whites and yolks with the cornmeal mixture and anise seed (if using).
8. Pour the batter into the prepared baking pan.
9. Bake in a preheated 400°F oven for 45 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
10. Serve hot, cut into squares.
NOTE: For a pleasant variation and slightly different texture, cube some of the Muenster cheese to measure 1/2 cup and stir it into the batter at the last moment.
